era_context: Old Babylonian period; Second Intermediate Period in Egypt; Indus Valley Civilization mature phase. Regional powers were consolidating control through military and diplomatic means. Long-distance trade networks connected Mesopotamia, the Indus Valley, Egypt, and the Levant. Writing systems were in use in Mesopotamia, Egypt, and the Indus Valley, though the latter remains undeciphered.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. (No events documented for this year.)
historiographic_note: 1903 BCE is not documented in any known historical source. The year is archaeologically indistinguishable from surrounding years. Historical knowledge of this period is reconstructed from broader chronological frameworks, not from year-specific records. The absence of documentation is typical for the early second millennium BCE outside of Egypt and Mesopotamia, and even within those regions, year-by-year records are rare.
